research_question: How can a lightweight CNN with fewer than 200K parameters achieve over 92.5% validation accuracy on FashionMNIST while maintaining fast training and inference speed?
background: FashionMNIST is a widely-used benchmark consisting of 28×28 grayscale images across 10 clothing categories, designed as a more challenging drop-in replacement for MNIST. Lightweight model design is critical for rapid experimental iteration in automated pipelines and resource-constrained deployment scenarios. Achieving high accuracy under strict parameter budgets requires careful architectural choices such as depthwise separable convolutions, efficient channel allocation, and appropriate regularization strategies.

image_analysis.input_characteristics: 28×28 single-channel grayscale images with centered clothing items on uniform backgrounds; low spatial resolution limits fine-grained texture information
image_analysis.target_characteristics: 10-class categorical label (T-shirt, Trouser, Pullover, Dress, Coat, Sandal, Shirt, Sneaker, Bag, Ankle boot)
image_analysis.visual_gap: Inter-class similarity is high for upper-body garments (T-shirt vs Shirt vs Pullover vs Coat) while intra-class variation exists in shape and style; low resolution compresses discriminative details

problem_analysis.core_problem: Designing a CNN architecture that maximizes classification accuracy on FashionMNIST under a strict 200K parameter budget with minimal training time
current_limitations: Simple shallow CNNs plateau around 90-91% accuracy due to insufficient representational capacity for visually similar classes
Standard efficient architectures (MobileNet, ShuffleNet) are over-parameterized for 28×28 input and waste capacity on unnecessary downsampling stages
Naively reducing model width or depth to meet parameter budgets leads to information bottlenecks and accuracy degradation
Regularization and data augmentation strategies optimized for large-scale datasets may not transfer well to small grayscale images
problem_analysis.why_difficult: FashionMNIST contains visually ambiguous classes (e.g., Shirt vs T-shirt vs Pullover) where discriminative features are subtle and localized, yet the strict parameter budget limits the model's capacity to learn rich hierarchical representations; balancing expressiveness with efficiency at such small scale is a non-trivial architectural design challenge.

in_scope: Custom lightweight CNN architecture design (≤200K parameters)
Depthwise separable convolutions and channel-efficient building blocks
Data augmentation strategies for 28×28 grayscale images
Regularization techniques (dropout, weight decay, label smoothing)
Learning rate scheduling and training recipe optimization
Ablation study across 2-3 iterative design refinements
Single-GPU training with short epoch budget
out_scope: Large pretrained backbones (ResNet-50, EfficientNet-B3+, ViT-Base, etc.)
Knowledge distillation from large teacher models
Neural architecture search (NAS) requiring massive compute
Non-CNN approaches (pure Transformer, MLP-Mixer) unless extremely lightweight
Post-training quantization or pruning as primary accuracy strategy

primary: depthwise separable convolution low-parameter image classification
compact CNN architecture grayscale clothing recognition
efficient small CNN design sub-200K parameters
secondary: channel shuffle inverted residual lightweight block
fast training convergence small dataset CNN

quantitative: Training completes within ~10 minutes on a single GPU
Progressive accuracy improvement across 2-3 experimental iterations
qualitative: Model architecture is clean, modular, and easily reproducible
Design decisions are justified through ablation or empirical comparison
Training pipeline is compatible with automated GitHub-based experiment loops
Inference latency remains at lightweight CNN level without special hardware

constraints: No large or complex pretrained backbones
Inference speed must remain at lightweight CNN level
All code must be generalizable and modular (no hardcoding)
expected_challenges: Discriminating visually similar upper-body garment classes (Shirt, T-shirt, Pullover, Coat) with limited model capacity — may require targeted architectural choices like attention or multi-scale feature fusion within the parameter budget
Finding the optimal depth-width trade-off under the 200K parameter ceiling — too shallow loses hierarchical features while too narrow creates information bottlenecks at 28×28 resolution

Preventing overfitting on the relatively small and low-resolution training set (60K samples) when the model is tuned aggressively for accuracy — requires careful regularization and augmentation without excessive training overhead

input.topic: FashionMNIST 28x28 grayscale 이미지 분류를 위한 경량 CNN 모델 설계
input.details: 작은 파라미터 수와 빠른 추론 속도를 유지하면서도 높은 분류 정확도를 달성하는 경량 CNN 모델을 설계한다.
input.problem_definition: 작은 모델은 표현력이 부족해서 정확도가 쉽게 떨어지며, FashionMNIST는 MNIST보다 클래스 간 형태가 비슷해 단순한 구조로는 성능 한계가 있다. GitHub 기반 자동 실험 루프에서 빠르게 반복 가능한 문제여야 하므로 학습 시간이 짧아야 한다.
input.desired_outcome: Validation Accuracy 92.5% 이상을 달성하는 경량 분류 모델을 얻고 싶다. 모델은 빠르게 학습되고 추론 가능해야 하며, 2~3회의 실험 수정(Path A)을 거쳐 성능이 점진적으로 개선되는 것이 바람직하다.
input.constraints: parameter budget 0.2M 이하, single GPU 환경에서 실행 가능, 학습 시간은 짧아야 함, 추론 속도는 가벼운 CNN 수준으로 유지, 과도하게 복잡한 backbone이나 대형 pretrained model 사용 금지

retrieval_plan.task_type: FashionMNIST 28x28 grayscale 이미지 분류를 위한 경량 CNN 모델 설계
